quickSummary: Good job! You effectively introduced yourself and maintained a steady speaking pace.
whatYouDidWell: You introduced yourself in a structured and easy-to-follow way
Your content feels relevant and meaningful
Your explanation of your interests is clear and specific
You maintained a consistent speaking pace overall

structureAnalysis.excerpt: You clearly explained your academic background and current interests.
structureAnalysis.feedback: This provides a solid foundation for others to connect with you.

structureAnalysis.excerpt: The introduction ends without a clear closing statement.
structureAnalysis.feedback: This is a great opportunity to leave a stronger impression.

recommendedActions.title: Filler words (um, uh, like)
recommendedActions.description: These appear when you are thinking about what to say next. This is completely normal. Instead of filling the silence, try taking a short pause. Pauses actually make you sound more confident.

recommendedActions.title: Stronger ending
recommendedActions.description: Your introduction would feel more complete with a simple closing sentence. For example: 'I'm excited to be here and look forward to learning with all of you.'

recommendedActions.title: Confidence in transitions
recommendedActions.description: When moving between ideas, take a brief pause before continuing.
